evals.prompt: Run an intent gap analysis on breakingb2b.com. I want to find every keyword we rank for where the page doesn't actually match what searchers want — so I can figure out what new content to build. Exclude branded terms. Focus on anything with 20+ impressions a month.
evals.expected_output: A structured report listing content opportunities discovered from GSC/Ahrefs data. Should include: site baseline (DR, traffic), a prioritised opportunity table with primary keyword/Global MSV/KD/gap type/current URL, detailed breakdowns for top opportunities with SERP evidence and suggested content to build, cannibalization fixes if any found, zero-traffic rankings section, and quick wins for existing pages.
expectations: Report includes a site baseline section with DR and organic traffic metrics
An opportunity table is produced with at least one content recommendation
Each opportunity includes the gap type (e.g. format mismatch, split intent, depth gap)
SERP evidence is cited for at least one opportunity (what content types rank #1 for target keywords)
At least one Ahrefs tool was used to validate keyword volume and KD
At least one GSC tool or Ahrefs site-explorer tool was used to pull keyword data
At least one ranking page was crawled to assess intent match
Branded terms are excluded from the recommendations
A quick wins section is present distinguishing page updates from new-content opportunities
Keyword volumes are labelled as Global MSV, not US-only

evals.prompt: I need to find what new blog posts or guides I should create. Can you look at my GSC keywords for samdunning.org and find where I'm ranking but the page clearly isn't built for that query? Like position 10-20 stuff where someone could write a proper piece and do much better.
evals.expected_output: A content opportunity report focused on positions 10-20 where page intent doesn't match keyword intent. Should identify specific keywords where a dedicated new page would outperform the current ranking URL, with SERP analysis showing what format Google expects. Output should be actionable — specific working titles and content formats to build, not just a list of keywords.
expectations: GSC or Ahrefs data is pulled for the specified domain (or its associated content site)
Focus is on positions 10-20 as requested
Opportunities specify what type of content to build (guide, listicle, comparison, etc.)
SERP format evidence is included (what currently ranks at #1 for target keywords)
Each opportunity has a suggested working title or slug
Supporting keyword cluster is identified for each opportunity
A clear distinction is made between 'new page needed' vs 'update existing page'

evals.prompt: Do a content gap analysis on my search console — domain is breakingb2b.com. I specifically want to know about any keywords where I have a commercial/landing page ranking but the SERP is clearly asking for informational content, and vice versa. Also flag any cannibalization you find. KD under 35 only please.
evals.expected_output: A focused report on intent-type mismatches (informational vs commercial page type mismatches) plus a cannibalization section. Should use cannibalization_check tool and serp-overview to verify intent type for each candidate. Filtered to KD ≤ 35. Output clearly separates commercial-page-for-informational-query gaps from informational-page-for-commercial-query gaps.
expectations: Cannibalization check is performed and results are reported
Intent-type mismatches are specifically identified (commercial page for informational query, or vice versa)
All recommendations are filtered to KD ≤ 35 as requested
serp-overview is used to confirm SERP intent type for flagged keywords
Cannibalization section lists specific URL pairs/groups to consolidate
Report differentiates between two mismatch directions: commercial page ranking for informational query, and informational page ranking for commercial query
